demo.name: forgejo/forgejo
demo.teaser: A self-hosted forge codebase that makes the product boundary itself easy to demonstrate.

metrics.label: Maintainer concentration
metrics.note: A modest core still anchors critical forge workflows.

metrics.label: Contributor expertise signals
metrics.note: UI, auth, federation, and actions each show repeat expert contributors.

metrics.label: Subsystem hotspots
metrics.note: Web UI, repository APIs, and actions runners drive most coordination cost.

dashboard.headline: Product dogfooding story: Forgejo shell outside, Vaglio semantics inside.
dashboard.narrative: This demo makes the platform story concrete: repository browsing and account UX stay Forgejo-native while Vaglio adds semantic change tracking, provenance, and analysis surfaces beside it.

history.note: The shell can demo well before the semantic boundary is fully explained.
Separating Forgejo-native and Vaglio-native surfaces reduces confusion and lowers stress.
The remaining heat sits in policy and provenance, not basic routing or host availability.

metrics.label: Branch stress
metrics.note: Most uncertainty is architectural rather than operational: where should the semantic boundary live?

metrics.label: History heat
metrics.note: Repository APIs, web UI, and actions automation carry most of the integration pressure.

metrics.label: Active-inference confidence
metrics.note: Boundary clarity reduces stress faster than raw code churn reduction in this demo.

stress.headline: Product-boundary stress is lowest where Forgejo stays native and Vaglio stays semantic.
stress.narrative: This demo frames stress as the cost of forcing one layer to impersonate the other. Heat is highest where repository APIs, UI expectations, and automation governance overlap.

hotspots.title: repository api path
hotspots.detail: Heat appears where Git-facing expectations must be translated into a richer internal model without confusing operators.

hotspots.title: web shell path
hotspots.detail: UI reuse is valuable, but stress rises if product boundaries are hidden instead of made explicit.

hotspots.title: actions governance path
hotspots.detail: Agent-scale automation needs provenance and policy hooks or the apparent convenience becomes latent operational debt.

hotspots.note: This is where Git-shaped clients meet a more expressive internal model.
UI chrome can be reused while Vaglio adds differentiated analysis.
A natural place to show agent-scale provenance and control loops.

expertise_signals.title: Self-hosting credibility
expertise_signals.detail: Investors can see the product demonstrated on the very kind of platform it complements.

expertise_signals.title: Boundary clarity
expertise_signals.detail: The shell shows which features remain Forgejo-native versus which become Vaglio-native.

provenance.title: Imported source + local semantics
provenance.detail: Public upstream stays recognizable while imported analysis preserves a `jj`-first internal history.

provenance.title: Decision traceability
provenance.detail: Overlay roundtable and review context on top of Forgejo pull-request flows.

import_steps.detail: Mirror forgejo/forgejo into a Forgejo-hosted demo namespace without bespoke setup.

import_steps.step: Translate Git edge to jj semantics
import_steps.detail: Project refs, pull requests, and merges through the Git↔jj gateway before analysis.
Expose maintainer concentration, expertise signals, hotspots, provenance overlays, and the first stress/heat surface on the same host.
